question.text: Scenario: Mr. Vikram Choudhary, aged 45, is an ultra-HNI with a total investment portfolio of Rs. 25 crore. His current allocation is: Equity Mutual Funds Rs. 8 crore, Fixed Deposits Rs. 5 crore, Direct Equity Rs. 5 crore, Real Estate Rs. 4 crore, Gold Rs. 1.5 crore, and Cash Rs. 1.5 crore. His annual income is Rs. 3 crore. His investment adviser, Ms. Neha Sharma, suggests allocating Rs. 3 crore (12% of portfolio) to Alternative Investment Funds for diversification. She proposes: Option 1 — Category I AIF (Venture Capital Fund): Invests in early-stage tech startups. Minimum Rs. 1 crore. Close-ended with 7-year lock-in. Target return 20-25% IRR. Manager continuing interest 2.5% of corpus. Fund corpus Rs. 200 crore with maximum 250 investors. Option 2 — Category II AIF (Private Equity Fund): Invests in mid-market growth companies. Minimum Rs. 1 crore. Close-ended with 5-year lock-in plus 2-year extension option. Target return 15-18% IRR. No leverage permitted except for day-to-day operations. Management fee 2% + carried interest 20% above 8% hurdle. Option 3 — Category III AIF (Long-Short Equity Hedge Fund): Employs long-short equity strategies using derivatives. Minimum Rs. 1 crore. Open-ended with 1-year lock-in. Target return 12-15% with low correlation to equity markets. Manager continuing interest 5% of corpus. Can employ leverage through derivatives. Vikram qualifies as an accredited investor (annual income ≥ Rs. 2 crore). Question 2 of 5: The Cat I AIF (Venture Capital Fund) has a fund corpus of Rs. 200 crore and maximum 250 investors. What is the minimum Manager/Sponsor continuing interest in this fund?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For Cat I and II AIFs, Manager/Sponsor continuing interest = 2.5% of corpus or Rs. 5 crore, whichever is LOWER. 2.5% of Rs. 200 crore = Rs. 5 crore. Rs. 5 crore limit cap = Rs. 5 crore. So minimum is Rs. 5 crore (both are equal in this case). **The Trap:** 5% / Rs. 10 crore applies to Category III, not Category I. **Cross-Exam Context:** AIF Manager continuing interest requirements by category are tested in X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Rs. 5 crore of Manager's own capital at risk provides meaningful 'skin in the game' — it aligns the Manager's interests with investors. Higher continuing interest percentage signals more confidence from the Manager.
